In 1779, during the American Revolutionary War, more than 800 Black and mixed-race volunteer soldiers from Saint-Domingue (present-day Haiti) took part in the fighting.
They fought in the
⚔️ Siege of Savannah
Date: October 9, 1779
Location: Savannah, Georgia
They fought alongside French troops against the
United Kingdom.
Historical records indicate that young officers such as
Henri Christophe
(then about 12–13 years old) were among the volunteers.
🇺🇸 What does this mean?
Haitians (then the colony of Saint-Domingue) provided military support to help the United States in its fight against the United Kingdom.
U.S. Independence: 1776
Battle of Savannah: 1779
Haiti’s Independence: 1804 under
Jean-Jacques Dessalines
Haitian ancestors contributed to the American War of Independence.
This is a historical pride for Haiti 🇭🇹.

Today, the Trump Administration is proud to announce the United States of America’s updated Childhood Vaccination Schedule. This Schedule is rooted in the Gold Standard of Science, and widely agreed upon by Scientists and Experts all over the World. Effective today, America will no longer require 72 “jabs” for our beautiful, healthy children. We are moving to a far more reasonable Schedule, where all children will only be recommended to receive Vaccinations for 11 of the most serious and dangerous diseases. Parents can still choose to give their children all of the Vaccinations, if they wish, and they will still be covered by insurance. However, this updated Schedule finally aligns the United States with other Developed Nations around the World. Congratulations to HHS Secretary Bobby Kennedy, CDC Acting Director Jim O’Neil, FDA Commissioner Marty Makary, CMS Administrator Dr. Oz, NIH Director Jay Bhattacharya, and all of the Medical Experts and Professionals who worked very hard to make this happen. Many Americans, especially the “MAHA Moms,” have been praying for these COMMON SENSE reforms for many years. Thank you for your attention to this matter!
 
DONALD J. TRUMP
PRESIDENT OF THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA

In 1779, during the American Revolutionary War, more than 800 Black and mixed-race volunteer soldiers from Saint-Domingue (present-day Haiti) took part in the fighting.
They fought in the
⚔️ Siege of Savannah
Date: October 9, 1779
Location: Savannah, Georgia
They fought alongside French troops against the
United Kingdom.
Historical records indicate that young officers such as
Henri Christophe
(then about 12–13 years old) were among the volunteers.
🇺🇸 What does this mean?
Haitians (then the colony of Saint-Domingue) provided military support to help the United States in its fight against the United Kingdom.
U.S. Independence: 1776
Battle of Savannah: 1779
Haiti’s Independence: 1804 under
Jean-Jacques Dessalines
Haitian ancestors contributed to the American War of Independence.
This is a historical pride for Haiti 🇭🇹.

MOTE CON HUESILLO
INGREDIENTES:
• 500 gr de huesillo
• 2 litros de agua hervida
• 2 palos de Canela Entera Gourmet
• ½ taza de azúcar rubia
• 1/3 paquete de chancaca
• 2 a 3 tazas de mote fresco
PREPARACIÓN:
• Lavar bien los huesillos y ponerlos con el agua hervida en un bol, tapar y dejar reposar toda la noche. Al otro día, poner los huesillos con el agua de remojo, Canela Entera Gourmet, azúcar y chancaca en una olla. Cocinar a fuego medio hasta que los huesillos estén cocidos, estarán blandos al pincharlos; 35 a 40 minutos. Enfriar y luego refrigerar hasta que estén bien helados.
• En otra olla, cocinar el mote en agua por 3 a 4 minutos. Colar y enfriar el mote bajo el chorro de agua fría. Reservar.
• Para servir, poner mote en el fondo de un vaso alto. Poner 3 a 4 huesillos y luego tapar con el jugo de los huesillos. Servir inmediatamente.
---

Dear Mr. President Trump,
My name is Ali, a simple worker from Iran.
Did you not once give the people of Iran hope for freedom? Why does it now feel as though that hope has diminished? Were the people of Iran not encouraged to stand up and claim their fundamental rights? Many Iranians have lost their lives believing that the international community — and especially the United States of America — would stand beside them.
Meanwhile, supporters of the ruling establishment continue to chant “Death to America” and “Death to Israel.” In various public gatherings, they burn the flags of the United States and Israel and openly speak of the destruction of these nations. Mr. Trump, we respectfully ask that, unlike certain previous approaches, you do not enter into negotiations with the Islamic Republic — which many Iranians view as responsible for the killing and repression of young people and innocent citizens — and that you do not, as was perceived under President Biden, grant concessions to the Islamic Republic that may strengthen a system many Iranians hold responsible for repression and violence.
We respectfully ask you to stand clearly and decisively with the people of Iran. If the United States does not openly support the Iranian people, concerns may arise that, for political or strategic reasons, it is indirectly contributing to the continuation of clerical rule.
The people of Iran seek freedom, human dignity, and genuine support from the international community. History will judge who stood with the Iranian nation at this critical moment.
Respectfully,
Ali

MOTE CON HUESILLO
INGREDIENTES:
• 500 gr de huesillo
• 2 litros de agua hervida
• 2 palos de Canela Entera Gourmet
• ½ taza de azúcar rubia
• 1/3 paquete de chancaca
• 2 a 3 tazas de mote fresco
PREPARACIÓN:
• Lavar bien los huesillos y ponerlos con el agua hervida en un bol, tapar y dejar reposar toda la noche. Al otro día, poner los huesillos con el agua de remojo, Canela Entera Gourmet, azúcar y chancaca en una olla. Cocinar a fuego medio hasta que los huesillos estén cocidos, estarán blandos al pincharlos; 35 a 40 minutos. Enfriar y luego refrigerar hasta que estén bien helados.
• En otra olla, cocinar el mote en agua por 3 a 4 minutos. Colar y enfriar el mote bajo el chorro de agua fría. Reservar.
• Para servir, poner mote en el fondo de un vaso alto. Poner 3 a 4 huesillos y luego tapar con el jugo de los huesillos. Servir inmediatamente.
---

In 1779, during the American Revolutionary War, more than 800 Black and mixed-race volunteer soldiers from Saint-Domingue (present-day Haiti) took part in the fighting.
They fought in the
⚔️ Siege of Savannah
Date: October 9, 1779
Location: Savannah, Georgia
They fought alongside French troops against the
United Kingdom.
Historical records indicate that young officers such as
Henri Christophe
(then about 12–13 years old) were among the volunteers.
🇺🇸 What does this mean?
Haitians (then the colony of Saint-Domingue) provided military support to help the United States in its fight against the United Kingdom.
U.S. Independence: 1776
Battle of Savannah: 1779
Haiti’s Independence: 1804 under
Jean-Jacques Dessalines
Haitian ancestors contributed to the American War of Independence.
This is a historical pride for Haiti 🇭🇹.

latestComments/7/text:
Mr. Donald Trump,

I write to you not as a politician,
but as a voice carrying the grief of countless Iranian families.

Just over a month ago, you told the people of Iran to go to the streets.
You said you were watching, and that help was on the way.
People believed those words.
They stepped outside with hope, not weapons.

Many of them never came back.

More than a month has passed.
For us, this month has been filled with fear, funerals, and silence.
While the world watches, the Islamic Republic continues to arrest, torture, and secretly execute its people.

Among the dead are 128 school students.
Please pause for a moment and consider what this means.
This is not a number.
This is four or five full classrooms—
desks that will never be used again,
notebooks left unfinished,
dreams stopped in childhood.

Families are informed late—or not at all.
Graves appear without names.
Final goodbyes are taken away.

We ask you, with deep respect and desperation:

If you are watching, please truly see us.
See the mothers who wait every night for voices that will never return.
See the children growing up without parents.
See the empty classrooms, the empty chairs, the lives that ended too soon.

These are not statistics.
They are human lives shaped by hope—and broken by waiting.

If help is coming, we beg you not to let it arrive too late.
Even one month can mean the difference between life and death.
Every day of delay costs another family everything.

We are not asking for miracles.
Only for urgency, compassion, and meaningful action.

Please do not let history remember this moment
as a time when cries for help were heard
but allowed to fade with time.

Respectfully,
A voice from the people of Iran

These are not statistics.

Even one month can mean the difference between life and death.
Every day of delay costs another family everything.

We are not asking for miracles.
Only for urgency, compassion, and meaningful action.

as a time when cries for help were heard
but allowed to fade with time.

Mr. Donald Trump,

I write to you not as a politician,
but as a voice carrying the grief of countless Iranian families.

Just over a month ago, you told the people of Iran to go to the streets.
You said you were watching, and that help was on the way.
People believed those words.
They stepped outside with hope, not weapons.

Many of them never came back.

More than a month has passed.
For us, this month has been filled with fear, funerals, and silence.
While the world watches, the Islamic Republic continues to arrest, torture, and secretly execute its people.

Among the dead are 128 school students.
Please pause for a moment and consider what this means.
This is not a number.
This is four or five full classrooms—
desks that will never be used again,
notebooks left unfinished,
dreams stopped in childhood.

Families are informed late—or not at all.
Graves appear without names.
Final goodbyes are taken away.

We ask you, with deep respect and desperation:

If you are watching, please truly see us.
See the mothers who wait every night for voices that will never return.
See the children growing up without parents.
See the empty classrooms, the empty chairs, the lives that ended too soon.

These are not statistics.
They are human lives shaped by hope—and broken by waiting.

If help is coming, we beg you not to let it arrive too late.
Even one month can mean the difference between life and death.
Every day of delay costs another family everything.

We are not asking for miracles.
Only for urgency, compassion, and meaningful action.

Please do not let history remember this moment
as a time when cries for help were heard
but allowed to fade with time.

Respectfully,
A voice from the people of Iran

Como cocinar cerdo: Antes de cocinar, es fundamental dejar la cabeza impecable:
Rasurado: Usa un rastrillo nuevo para eliminar cualquier rastro de pelo en la piel, trompa y orejas.
Lavado: Frota con una escobeta, agua y jabón neutro (enjuagando muy bien) para quitar impurezas y restos de sangre.
Corte: Realiza un corte profundo en la parte inferior hasta el hueso para que el calor penetre mejor y se reduzca el tiempo de cocción. 
￼YouTube +1
2. Primera Cocción: El Hervido
Este paso ablanda la carne y la impregna de sabor.
Ingredientes del caldo: En una olla grande con suficiente agua para cubrir la cabeza, añade cebolla, ajos, laurel, pimienta negra, orégano, sal y, opcionalmente, hierba buena o pimentón.
Tiempo: Cocina a fuego medio durante 1 a 3 horas. Estará lista cuando al enterrar un tenedor, este entre y salga con facilidad. 
￼YouTube +1
3. Segunda Cocción: El Dorado al Horno
Para ese acabado profesional de "sándwich" o "tacos callejeros":
Precalentado: Enciende el horno a 175°C - 180°C.
Barnizado: Pincela la piel con aceite de achiote o una mezcla de naranja agria, ajo y especias. Esto le dará un color dorado rojizo muy atractivo.
Horneado: Colócala en una fuente y hornea de 30 a 60 minutos hasta que la piel esté crujiente y dorada. 
￼YouTube +2
Ideas para servir
Tacos o Sándwiches: Desmenuza la carne de los cachetes (la más sabrosa) y sírvela con una salsa criolla (cebolla, limón y ají) o salsa verde.
Guarnición: Acompaña con papas doradas, camote o arroz blanco

In 1779, during the American Revolutionary War, more than 800 Black and mixed-race volunteer soldiers from Saint-Domingue (present-day Haiti) took part in the fighting.
They fought in the
⚔️ Siege of Savannah
Date: October 9, 1779
Location: Savannah, Georgia
They fought alongside French troops against the
United Kingdom.
Historical records indicate that young officers such as
Henri Christophe
(then about 12–13 years old) were among the volunteers.
🇺🇸 What does this mean?
Haitians (then the colony of Saint-Domingue) provided military support to help the United States in its fight against the United Kingdom.
U.S. Independence: 1776
Battle of Savannah: 1779
Haiti’s Independence: 1804 under
Jean-Jacques Dessalines
Haitian ancestors contributed to the American War of Independence.

⚔️ Siege of Savannah

Location: Savannah, Georgia
They fought alongside French troops against the
United Kingdom.

Henri Christophe

Haitians (then the colony of Saint-Domingue) provided military support to help the United States in its fight against the United Kingdom.

Haiti’s Independence: 1804 under
Jean-Jacques Dessalines

👏Dear Mr. Donald Trump,
I am writing this open letter with due respect for your public efforts in defense of full democracy in the countries of the Americas, and to pose, sincerely and directly, a question that I believe is of great importance.
Regarding Venezuela—a nation endowed with vast natural resources yet profoundly afflicted by political, economic, and humanitarian crises—I seek clear understanding on the following: Do your efforts, and those of your administration, have as their primary objective the restoration of full democracy in Venezuela, with free and fair elections, respect for human rights, and independent institutions, thereby enabling the Venezuelan people to determine their own destiny in sovereign fashion? Or, conversely, is the true priority to secure access to Venezuelan oil in order to enrich the United States treasury, by exploiting what is effectively the only significant revenue source the Venezuelan people possess for rebuilding their essential infrastructure—roads, hospitals, and schools?
I believe that a frank answer to this question would greatly help to clarify true intentions and bolster the confidence of many international observers in your administration’s foreign policy. Democracy must not be used as a pretext for unilateral economic interests; rather, it should reflect a genuine commitment to the sovereignty and well-being of nations and their peoples.
I thank you in advance for your attention and for the opportunity this open dialogue represents.
